text: Progression-free survival was significantly longer in the pembrolizumab group than in the chemotherapy group (hazard ratio for disease progression or death, 0.50; 95% CI, 0.37 to 0.68; P<0.001).
The hazard ratio for death was 0.49 (95% CI, 0.38 to 0.64; P<0.001) in the pembrolizumab-combination group.
The hazard ratio for death or disease progression was 0.42 (99.5% CI, 0.31 to 0.57; P<0.001) with nivolumab plus ipilimumab.
Overall survival was significantly longer with atezolizumab plus bevacizumab plus chemotherapy (hazard ratio for death, 0.78; 95% CI, 0.64 to 0.96; P=0.02).
The hazard ratio for progression or death in the pertuzumab group was 0.62 (95% CI, 0.51 to 0.75; P<0.001).
Median progression-free survival was 24.8 months with palbociclib-letrozole versus 14.5 months with placebo-letrozole (hazard ratio, 0.58; 95% CI, 0.46 to 0.72; P<0.001).
The hazard ratio for disease progression or death was 0.56 (95% CI, 0.43 to 0.72; P<0.001) with ribociclib.
Progression-free survival was significantly longer with olaparib than with placebo (hazard ratio for disease progression or death, 0.53; 95% CI, 0.35 to 0.82; P=0.004).
The hazard ratio for disease progression or death was 0.58 (95% CI, 0.43 to 0.80; P<0.001) with olaparib.
The hazard ratio for disease progression or death was 0.47 (95% CI, 0.34 to 0.65; P<0.001) with alectinib.
The hazard ratio for disease progression or death was 0.52 (95% CI, 0.42 to 0.65; P<0.001) with durvalumab.
The hazard ratio for disease progression or death was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.56 to 0.84; P<0.001) with avelumab plus axitinib.
The hazard ratio for death was 0.53 (95% CI, 0.38 to 0.74; P<0.001) with pembrolizumab plus axitinib.
Progression-free survival was longer with atezolizumab plus nab-paclitaxel (hazard ratio for progression or death, 0.80; 95% CI, 0.69 to 0.92; P=0.002).
The hazard ratio for disease progression or death was 0.28 (95% CI, 0.22 to 0.37; P<0.001) with trastuzumab deruxtecan.
The hazard ratio for radiographic progression or death was 0.40 (99.2% CI, 0.29 to 0.57; P<0.001) with lutetium-177-PSMA-617.
The hazard ratio for imaging-based progression or death was 0.34 (95% CI, 0.25 to 0.47; P<0.001) with olaparib.
Progression-free survival was significantly longer with ceritinib than with chemotherapy (hazard ratio 0.55, 95% CI 0.42-0.73; p<0.0001).
The hazard ratio for disease progression or death was 0.46 (95% CI, 0.37 to 0.57; P<0.001) with osimertinib.
The hazard ratio for disease recurrence or death was 0.20 (99.12% CI, 0.14 to 0.30; P<0.001) with osimertinib.
